user.title: Combined Notifications
user.description: Create a new notification sensor. You can configure conditions and appearance after setup.

data_description.name: Lowercase, underscores only, no spaces. This becomes your sensor entity ID.
data_description.friendly_sensor_name: Optional — shown in dashboards instead of the sensor name.

error.already_configured: A sensor with this name already exists.
error.invalid_name: Name must contain at least one valid character (letters, numbers, underscores).
error.entity_already_exists: This sensor name already exists in Home Assistant. Please choose a different name.

init.title: Combined Notifications Settings
init.description:
If the configuration panel appears blank, enable compatibility mode. Note: compatibility mode disables real-time entity updates and the native HA icon picker.

⚠️ BREAKING CHANGE — Attribute mode: if you enable this, your existing dashboard cards and automations WILL BREAK. You must update them to reference the attribute instead of the sensor state directly. Use state_attr('sensor.YOUR_SENSOR_NAME', 'alert_list') in place of states('sensor.YOUR_SENSOR_NAME').

data.use_attributes: Enable attribute mode (recommended for large setups)

data_description.compatibility_mode: Use if your configuration panel appears blank.
data_description.use_attributes: When enabled: sensor state becomes on/off, full alert list moves to alert_list attribute (no 255 character limit). Default is off — existing automations and cards are unaffected.
